medications: Naproxen 250 mg as needed with food (back pain flare-ups)
health.mental_health: After being laid off from a long-term job and starting the bakery, Quinn has periods of anxiety and low mood tied to cash-flow pressure and fear of failing; sleep is patchy and they avoid alcohol and late meals to reduce reflux.
recent_visits: GP review for reflux symptoms and medication adjustment (last 3 months)
Physiotherapy sessions for chronic lower-back pain (ongoing, fortnightly)
NHS blood test for thyroid function (TSH/T4) and dose review (last 2 months)
Occupational health-style assessment via private physio for safe lifting/standing at work (last 6 months)

finance.income_range: Approx. £30,000–£55,000/year personal take-home equivalent (variable; depends on bakery trading and seasonality)
finance.debt: Carries a small-business loan (~£38,000 remaining) used for fit-out and ovens; also has ~£6,500 on a 0% balance-transfer credit card from the transition period after redundancy, with repayments tightly scheduled.
investments: Workplace pension pot from previous employer (paused contributions)
Stocks & Shares ISA (small, mostly index funds)
recent_transactions: Monthly rent payment for the bakery unit in East London
Supplier invoice paid to a flour mill and dairy wholesaler
Equipment maintenance call-out for a convection oven
Redundancy payout remainder transferred into business account as working capital
Quarterly VAT payment set aside and partially paid
Payment to an accountant for year-end accounts and payroll setup

relationships: Partner: Samira Khan (lives together; helps with weekend markets and admin)
Close friend: Elliot Price (ex-colleague from the job they were laid off from; emotional support and occasional mentoring)
Sibling: Nadia Al-Rashid (lives in Manchester; supportive but worries about financial risk)
Neighbour/friend: Mo Chen (local; swaps favours like pet-sitting and helps with deliveries when needed)
social.social_media_activity: Uses Instagram and TikTok for the bakery (posting daily bakes, behind-the-scenes early mornings, and weekend specials). Keeps personal accounts locked down; avoids posting about health or finances, but occasionally shares general reflections about career change and resilience.
private_conversations: Discussing the circumstances of the layoff and whether it was handled fairly
Arguments with partner about how much personal savings to put into the bakery
Confiding in a friend about anxiety, sleep issues, and feeling overwhelmed by responsibility
Talking with sibling about contingency plans if the bakery can’t cover rent over winter

schedule.work_hours: Typically Tue–Sat 05:00–15:00 for production and front-of-house; admin and ordering Mon 09:30–12:30; occasional Sun 07:00–12:00 for pop-ups/markets (seasonal).
regular_appointments: Thyroid blood test follow-up / GP check-in (every 3–6 months)
Monthly meeting/call with accountant (first Monday of the month, 14:00)
Wholesale order placement window with main supplier (twice weekly, evenings)
upcoming_events: Hackney weekend market stall booking (next Saturday 08:00–14:00)
Food hygiene refresher course (online, next Tuesday 18:30)
Landlord inspection of the bakery unit (in two weeks, 10:00)
Supplier tasting meeting for new vegan pastry line (next Friday 15:30)
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 04:10, quick tea and a small snack that won’t trigger reflux, bake-off and prep from early morning, short break mid-morning, service until early afternoon, then cleaning, stock counts, and ordering; evenings are mostly recovery, stretching for back pain, and early bed when possible.

professional.company: Independent neighbourhood bakery and coffee counter operating as a UK limited company (Ltd) with a small production kitchen and takeaway front
projects: Launching a weekday breakfast bun and coffee deal to boost morning footfall
Developing a low-acid menu board (reflux-friendly options) for personal experimentation and customer interest
Implementing a new POS and inventory system to reduce waste
Hiring and training a part-time barista for Saturdays
Negotiating a supply contract for flour to lock in pricing
professional.performance_issues: Occasional missed admin deadlines (invoicing, supplier reconciliation) during weeks when back pain flares or when sleep is poor; Quinn worries this could lead to cash-flow surprises and is trying to standardise processes.
workplace_relationships: Reliable relationship with head baker (Marta Lewandowska) but tension when Quinn pushes for new product trials during busy weeks
Friendly rapport with neighbouring businesses on the parade, including a greengrocer who refers customers
Some friction with the landlord’s property manager over repairs and service-charge queries

hobbies: Recipe testing and bread-scoring practice at home
Walking along the Regent’s Canal to decompress
Listening to podcasts about small business and food culture
Gardening herbs on a small balcony (mint, thyme, rosemary)
Occasional museum evenings (Tate Modern, V&A) when energy allows
habits: Avoids late-night meals and acidic foods to manage GERD; keeps antacid in bag
Daily back stretches and heat pack use after shifts
Drinks 3–5 coffees on workdays but tries to switch to decaf after noon due to reflux and sleep
Tracks sales and waste in a spreadsheet most evenings (can become obsessive when anxious)
Uses nicotine gum intermittently during high-stress periods (trying to stop)
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom flat in East London with their partner; the second room doubles as a quiet admin space and occasional storage for non-perishables when the bakery is tight on space.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly Mediterranean-leaning at home with an emphasis on smaller portions; limits spicy and tomato-heavy meals due to reflux; not strictly vegetarian but eats plant-forward meals and is experimenting with dairy-free pastries for the shop.
